name: spo2-dial
title: SpO₂ Dial
description: Compact arc gauge for oxygen saturation with normal/low/critical threshold bands.

props.name: supplemental
props.description: Flag that the reading is on supplemental oxygen.

clinicalNotes: Bands follow common thresholds: ≥95% normal, 90–94% low, <90% critical — but targets differ for COPD and other chronic hypoxia, so pass `band` to override. Pulse oximetry has real accuracy caveats: it can overestimate saturation in patients with darker skin tones and reads poorly with low perfusion, motion, or nail polish, so this displays the measured value and flags a low reading rather than hiding it — a dubious low SpO₂ is a prompt to check the patient and the probe, not to suppress. The supplemental-oxygen flag matters because 94% on room air and 94% on 4L are very different. The value and band are announced as text, so status is never conveyed by color alone; critical pulses (reduced-motion safe).
notes: medcn-original. SVG 270° arc gauge from primitives; faint band zones + value arc + marker.
